description: PyTorch binary image classification with GPU support and Google Colab notebook

tech_stack.dataset: Oxford-IIIT Pet Dataset (public, auto-download via torchvision)

tasks.title: Environment Setup
steps: Create a Python 3.10+ virtual environment
Run: pip install -r requirements.txt
Verify GPU with: python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available())"
acceptance_criteria: torch.cuda.is_available() returns True on GPU machine
All packages import without error

tasks.title: Dataset Preparation
tasks.description: Obtain the Oxford-IIIT Pet Dataset (public, free, auto-download)
steps: Dataset downloads automatically on first training run (~750 MB)
Stored in ./data/oxford-iiit-pet/
Binary labels: Cat=0, Dog=1 (derived from species tag)
acceptance_criteria: OxfordIIITPet downloads without error
DataLoader returns (B, 3, 224, 224) tensors with float labels 0.0 or 1.0
Train/Val split: ~80/20 of 7,349 total images

tasks.title: Model Definition & Architecture
tasks.description: Define CNN model architectures for binary classification
steps: Implement CatDogCNN: 4 Conv blocks + GAP + classifier head
Implement ResNet-18 transfer learning variant
Implement ResNet-50 transfer learning variant
Expose build_model() factory function
acceptance_criteria: model.py imports without error
build_model('resnet18')(torch.randn(4,3,224,224)).shape == (4, 1)
Trainable param count printed on run

tasks.title: GPU Training Script
tasks.description: Train the model on GPU with full training loop
steps: Run: python train.py --model resnet18 --epochs 15
Monitor loss and accuracy each epoch
Best model saved to ./output/best_model.pth
Training history saved to ./output/training_history.json
Training curves plot saved to ./output/training_curves.png
acceptance_criteria: Training loop runs without error on CUDA GPU
Validation accuracy >= 90% after 15 epochs with ResNet-18
best_model.pth file created
training_curves.png generated

tasks.title: Single Image Prediction
tasks.description: Run inference on any cat or dog image
steps: Load best_model.pth checkpoint
Run: python predict.py --image path/to/image.jpg
Output shows label (Cat/Dog) and confidence percentage
acceptance_criteria: predict.py loads checkpoint without error
Output format: label + confidence + per-class probabilities
Works with JPEG and PNG files

tasks.title: Google Colab Jupyter Notebook
tasks.description: End-to-end notebook for Google Colab with free T4 GPU
steps: Upload CatsAndDogs_Colab.ipynb to Google Drive
Open in Google Colab
Set Runtime > Change Runtime > GPU (T4)
Run All Cells (Ctrl+F9)
acceptance_criteria: All cells run without error on Colab T4 GPU
Dataset downloads automatically in Colab
Model trains and achieves >90% val accuracy
Inference demo cell shows correct predictions

tasks.title: Hyperparameter Tuning (Optional)
steps: Try --model resnet50 for higher accuracy
Increase --epochs to 30
Adjust --lr 0.00005 for finer tuning
Add --batch-size 64 if GPU VRAM allows
acceptance_criteria: Validation accuracy > 95% achievable with ResNet-50

tasks.title: Data Preview — 2×2 Grid (Images 6–9 with Labels)
tasks.description: Display a 2×2 grid of dataset images (indices 6–9) with their Cat/Dog labels as a sanity check that the dataset and labels are loaded correctly
steps: Load the training DataLoader from dataset.py
Extract images at dataset indices 6, 7, 8, 9
Reverse the ImageNet normalisation (mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406], std=[0.229,0.224,0.225]) so pixels render correctly
Create a 2×2 matplotlib figure with plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(6, 6))
Display each image with ax.imshow() and set the title to 'Cat' or 'Dog' based on the label tensor
Save the figure to ./output/data_preview.png and call plt.show()
acceptance_criteria: 2×2 grid renders 4 distinct pet images without distortion
Each subplot title shows the correct species label (Cat or Dog)
output/data_preview.png is saved successfully
Script completes without requiring a trained model checkpoint

tasks.title: Training Visualization — Loss & Accuracy Curves
tasks.description: Plot training and validation loss alongside training and validation accuracy over all epochs from the saved training history JSON
steps: Load ./output/training_history.json which contains per-epoch train_loss, val_loss, train_acc, val_acc lists
Create a 1×2 matplotlib figure (figsize=(12, 4))
Left subplot: plot train_loss and val_loss vs epoch with legend, xlabel='Epoch', ylabel='Loss', title='Loss Curves'
Right subplot: plot train_acc and val_acc vs epoch with legend, xlabel='Epoch', ylabel='Accuracy (%)', title='Accuracy Curves'
Mark the best validation epoch with a vertical dashed line on both subplots
Save the figure to ./output/training_curves.png (overwrite if exists) and call plt.show()
acceptance_criteria: Both subplots render with correctly labeled axes and legends
Curves are smooth and match the values in training_history.json
Best-epoch marker is visible on both subplots
output/training_curves.png is saved successfully
Script exits cleanly if training_history.json does not exist and prints a helpful error message

tasks.title: Confusion Matrix
tasks.description: Run the best model over the full validation split, collect predictions vs ground-truth labels, and render a labelled confusion matrix heatmap
steps: Load best_model.pth checkpoint and set model to eval mode
Iterate over the validation DataLoader (no gradient computation) and accumulate predicted labels (threshold sigmoid output at 0.5) and true labels
Compute the 2×2 confusion matrix with sklearn.metrics.confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred)
Plot with seaborn.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt='d', cmap='Blues', xticklabels=['Cat','Dog'], yticklabels=['Cat','Dog'])
Add title 'Confusion Matrix — Validation Set', xlabel='Predicted', ylabel='Actual'
Print overall accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score using sklearn.metrics.classification_report
Save the figure to ./output/confusion_matrix.png and call plt.show()
acceptance_criteria: Confusion matrix is a 2×2 integer grid with Cat and Dog axis labels
Printed classification report shows precision/recall/F1 per class
output/confusion_matrix.png is saved successfully
Script handles missing checkpoint gracefully with a clear error message

tasks.title: Unknown Image Prediction — Inference + Confidence Chart
tasks.description: Accept any image path (cat, dog, or unknown), run inference with the trained model, display the image alongside a horizontal bar chart showing Cat vs Dog confidence scores
steps: Accept an image path via CLI argument --image or hardcode a test path for notebook use
Apply the same preprocessing as training: Resize(256) → CenterCrop(224) → ToTensor() → Normalize(ImageNet stats)
Load best_model.pth, run a forward pass, apply sigmoid to get P(Dog); derive P(Cat) = 1 − P(Dog)
Determine the predicted label: Dog if P(Dog) ≥ 0.5 else Cat
Create a 1×2 matplotlib figure (figsize=(10, 4))
Left subplot: display the original image with title 'Input Image'
Right subplot: horizontal bar chart with bars for Cat and Dog, values as percentages, colour the winning bar green and the losing bar red; add a text label on each bar showing the percentage
Print a summary line: 'Result: <Label>  (<confidence>% confident)'
Save the figure to ./output/prediction_<imagename>.png and call plt.show()
acceptance_criteria: Script accepts any JPEG or PNG image without errors
Left subplot shows the original (un-normalised) image correctly oriented
Right subplot horizontal bar chart shows two bars labelled Cat and Dog with percentage values
Winning class bar is green; losing class bar is red
Printed result line matches the bar chart prediction
output/prediction_<imagename>.png is saved successfully

milestones.name: Full Pipeline Working
milestones.deliverable: Inference working, Colab notebook fully functional

milestones.name: Visualization Suite Complete
milestones.deliverable: Data preview grid, training curves, confusion matrix, and confidence chart all generated
